changes_made: Changed k8s_networkpolicy_tls_enforced to k8s_networkpolicy_tls_required for clarity
Changed k8s_service_mesh_mtls_enabled to k8s_servicemesh_mtls_enabled for consistency with naming conventions
review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the control SC-8(4) for ensuring transmission confidentiality and integrity through TLS enforcement. Naming conventions mostly follow the required pattern, but minor adjustments were made for clarity and consistency. The functions accurately reflect Kubernetes security controls related to TLS and mTLS.
naming_improvements: Use 'required' instead of 'enforced' for network policies to better indicate the necessity of TLS
Ensure consistent use of 'servicemesh' as a single word for resource naming
title: Transmission Confidentiality And Integrity - Transmission confidentiality and integrity | conceal or randomize communications
step1_reasoning: The control SC-8(4) focuses on ensuring transmission confidentiality and integrity by concealing or randomizing communications. In Kubernetes, this can be achieved through enforcing TLS for network communications, both at the ingress level and within the service mesh. NetworkPolicies can be used to enforce TLS for pod-to-pod communication, while ingress controllers should have TLS enabled to secure external communications. Additionally, enabling mutual TLS (mTLS) in service meshes like Istio or Linkerd ensures secure communication between services. For etcd, enabling TLS ensures secure communication between the API server and etcd.
